title: Terrorism and Illicit Finance
summary: This source is a high-level overview page from the U.S. Treasury Department describing its mission to combat terrorism and illicit finance through the Office of Terrorism and Financial Intelligence. The page outlines various programs including sanctions enforcement (SDN list, OFAC), anti-money laundering efforts, asset forfeiture, and the Terrorist Finance Tracking Program. This is an informational landing page that does not contain any specific macro catalyst events, market data, or policy decisions relevant to financial market analysis.
key_points: The Office of Terrorism and Financial Intelligence combines Treasury's intelligence and enforcement functions to safeguard the financial system
Treasury implements economic sanctions against foreign threats and targets financial support networks of national security threats
Sanctions programs include the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list, counter-terrorism sanctions, and counter-narcotics sanctions
The Financial Action Task Force is an international body setting standards for combating money laundering and terrorist financing
The Treasury Forfeiture Fund receives assets from forfeited criminal enterprises
The Terrorist Finance Tracking Program (TFTP) has provided leads for preventing terrorist attacks
why_it_matters: This page provides no actionable information for market participants. It is a static overview page describing Treasury's general authorities and programs for national security purposes. It does not announce new sanctions, provide economic forecasts, or discuss policy implications that would constitute a macro catalyst.
